name: Retune The Clean Compact PTX Launch Bounds To Target Three-CTA Residency

summary.name: Retune The Clean Compact PTX Launch Bounds To Target Three-CTA Residency
summary.hypothesis: The compact PTX anchor is now stable again, but it still sits at 201 regs/thread with occupancy limited to 2 CTAs/SM. Tightening only the PTX microkernel launch-bounds target from 2 to 3 may trim enough live state to improve latency hiding without changing grouped rows, stage depth, or the wait/sync cadence.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Register-pressure-driven CTA residency remains the cleanest unresolved local bottleneck on the restored compact PTX surface.
summary.risk: Moderate. The code edit is tiny, but forcing a tighter register budget can trade one latency problem for spills or a weaker instruction schedule.
ranking_notes: This is the narrowest unresolved probe left on the live anchor because it changes one compile-time constraint and nothing else.
It directly targets the standing `launch__occupancy_limit_registers = 2.0` signal without reopening families that already failed on nearby compact PTX surfaces.
Frontier should prefer this over stale restore candidates because the branch no longer needs recovery; it needs one last bounded local test.
summary.stop_condition: Stop immediately if the next build or NCU run shows spill-driven fallout, registers stay flat while runtime regresses, or barrier/long_scoreboard both worsen.
expected_local_changes: Change only the compact PTX microkernel `__launch_bounds__(128, 2)` annotation to target 3 CTAs/SM.
Leave grouped_rows=4, the clean wait/sync cadence, stage depth, and dispatch routing unchanged.

notes: Node C may now implement this one candidate.
